frameworks.name: program induction
frameworks.one_line: Social learning formalized as inferring a probabilistic program from observations.

frameworks.name: Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: Learners infer latent causal programs from observed examples probabilistically.

frameworks.name: cultural learning
frameworks.one_line: Cumulative culture framed via high-fidelity intergenerational transmission.

core_question: How do humans achieve high-fidelity social learning that supports cumulative culture?
key_assumptions: Causal processes can be represented as probabilistic programs
Learners observe not only inputs and outputs but also the execution trace
Program induction proceeds by Bayesian inference over latent programs
manipulations: Presence vs absence of an observed execution trace alongside input-output pairs
Single-example learning conditions across two behavioral experiments
key_findings: Observing an execution trace speeds up program induction even from a single example
Human judgments are structured by the execution trace as predicted by the model
Behavior tracks the model's predictions across two experiments
interpretations: Execution-trace observation underlies high-fidelity human social learning
Program induction unifies emulation and imitation within one mechanism
